Package org.eventb.pp

Class PPProof

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.pp.PPProof
public class PPProof extends Object
This class is responsible for running PP on a given set of hypotheses and a goal.

Methods in this class must be called in a certain order. If the original set of hypotheses and the goal are in set theory, translate() must first be invoked to translate them into predicate calculus. Then, the loader must be invoked using load() and then the prover is invoked using prove(long). Once the prover is done, the result of the proof can be retrieved by using getResult(). To cancel a proof, method #cancel().

Since:
0.2

  • Field Details

    • DEBUG

      public static boolean DEBUG
      Debug flag for PROVER_TRACE

    • translateHook

      public static Runnable translateHook
      Hook codes to be run at the end of each phase. Reserved for testing purpose only.

    • loadHook

      public static Runnable loadHook

  • Method Details

    • debug

      public static void debug(String message)

    • getResult

      public PPResult getResult()
      Returns the result of this proof.
      Returns:
      the result of this proof

    • getClauses

      public Collection<Clause> getClauses()

    • translate

      public void translate()
      Invokes the translator. Translates the original hypotheses and goal to predicate calculus.

    • load

      public void load()
      Invokes the loader. Transforms the set of hypotheses and the goal into the CNF required as an input to the prover.

    • prove

      public void prove(long maxSteps)
      Invokes the prover. Tries to prove the current sequent in maximum maxSteps steps. Also, the prover will stop when the monitor indicates that it has been canceled.
      Parameters:
      maxSteps - maximal number of steps, or -1 to denote an infinite number
